page.title: Six heavens of the desire realm

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{buddhism}} '''六欲天'''（ろくよくてん）は、[[天部]]（神）のうち、いまだ欲望に捉われる6つの天界をいう。六天ともいう...'
revision.text:
{{buddhism}} '''六欲天'''（ろくよくてん）は、[[天部]]（神）のうち、いまだ欲望に捉われる6つの天界をいう。六天ともいう。またそのうちの最高位・[[他化自在天]]を特に指して言う場合もある。
他化自在天は、[[天魔]]波旬（てんま・はじゅん）の住処であることから、[[織田信長]]は「六欲天の魔王」と自称したといわれる。
== 概略 == [[仏教]]では、[[六道]]（[[地獄界]]・[[餓鬼界]]・[[畜生界]]・[[修羅界]]・[[人間界]]・[[天上界]]）、また十界（六道の上に[[声聞界]]・[[縁覚界]]・[[菩薩界]]・[[仏界]]を加えたもの）といった世界観がある。
このうち、六道の地獄から人間までは欲望に捉われた世界、つまり[[欲界]]という。しかし天上界では細部に分けられ、上に行くほど欲を離れ、物質的な[[色界]]・そして精神的な[[無色界]]（これを[[三界]]という）がある。
ただし、天上界の中でも人間界に近い下部の6つの天は、依然として欲望に束縛される世界であるため、これを'''六欲天'''という。
六欲天を上から記載すると次の通りとなる。
*[[他化自在天]]（たけじざいてん） :欲界の最高位。また天界の第6天、[[天魔]]波旬の住所。 *[[化楽天]]（けらくてん、楽変化天＝らくへんげてん、とも） :六欲天の第5天。この天に住む者は、自己の対境（五境）を変化して娯楽の境とする。 *[[兜率天]]（とそつてん、覩史多天＝としたてん、とも） :六欲天の第4天。[[須弥山]]の頂上、12[[由旬]]の処にある。 *[[夜摩天]]（やまてん、焔摩天＝えんまてん、とも） :六欲天の第3天。時に随って快楽を受くる世界。 *[[忉利天]]（とうりてん、三十三天＝さんじゅうさんてん、とも） :六欲天の第2天。須弥山の頂上、閻浮提の上、8万由旬の処にある。[[帝釈天]]のいる場所。 *[[四天王]]（してんのう、四大王衆天＝しだいおうしゅてん、とも） :六欲天の第1天。[[持国天]]・[[増長天]]・[[広目天]]・[[多聞天]]の四天王がいる場所。
== 関連項目 == *[[天部]] *[[三界]] *[[天魔]]
[[Category:仏教|ろくよくてん]]

{{buddhism}} '''六欲天'''（ろくよくてん）は、[[天部]]（神）のうち、いまだ欲望に捉われる6つの天界をいう。六天ともいう。またそのうちの最高位・[[他化自在天]]を特に指して言う場合もある。
他化自在天は、[[天魔]]波旬（てんま・はじゅん）の住処であることから、[[織田信長]]は「六欲天の魔王」と自称したといわれる。

ただし、天上界の中でも人間界に近い下部の6つの天は、依然として欲望に束縛される世界であるため、これを'''六欲天'''という。
六欲天を上から記載すると次の通りとなる。
*[[他化自在天]]（たけじざいてん） :欲界の最高位。また天界の第6天、[[天魔]]波旬の住所。 *[[化楽天]]（けらくてん、楽変化天＝らくへんげてん、とも） :六欲天の第5天。この天に住む者は、自己の対境（五境）を変化して娯楽の境とする。 *[[兜率天]]（とそつてん、覩史多天＝としたてん、とも） :六欲天の第4天。[[須弥山]]の頂上、12[[由旬]]の処にある。 *[[夜摩天]]（やまてん、焔摩天＝えんまてん、とも） :六欲天の第3天。時に随って快楽を受くる世界。 *[[忉利天]]（とうりてん、三十三天＝さんじゅうさんてん、とも） :六欲天の第2天。須弥山の頂上、閻浮提の上、8万由旬の処にある。[[帝釈天]]のいる場所。 *[[四天王]]（してんのう、四大王衆天＝しだいおうしゅてん、とも） :六欲天の第1天。[[持国天]]・[[増長天]]・[[広目天]]・[[多聞天]]の四天王がいる場所。
== 関連項目 == *[[天部]] *[[三界]] *[[天魔]]
[[Category:仏教|ろくよくてん]]

revision.comment: 天上界の名称は仏教で普遍的に知られるため、カテゴリー先を張り替えるのではなく追加する方が望ましいでしょう

'''六欲天'''（ろくよくてん）は、[[天 (仏教)|天上界]]のうち、いまだ欲望に捉われる6つの天界をいう。六天ともいう。またそのうちの最高位・[[他化自在天]]を特に指して言う場合もある。
他化自在天は、[[天魔]]波旬（てんま・はじゅん）の住処であることから、[[織田信長]]は「六欲天の魔王」と自称したといわれる。
== 概略 == [[仏教]]では、[[六道]]（[[地獄 (仏教)|地獄界]]・[[餓鬼|餓鬼界]]・[[畜生|畜生界]]・[[修羅道|修羅界]]・[[人間界]]・[[天上界]]）、また十界（六道の上に[[声聞|声聞界]]・[[縁覚|縁覚界]]・[[菩薩|菩薩界]]・[[仏|仏界]]を加えたもの）といった世界観がある。
このうち、六道の地獄から人間までは欲望に捉われた世界、つまり[[欲界]]という。しかし天上界では細部に分けられ、上に行くほど欲を離れ、物質的な[[色界]]・そして精神的な[[無色界]]（これを[[三界]]という）がある。
ただし、天上界の中でも人間界に近い下部の6つの天は、依然として欲望に束縛される世界であるため三界の中の欲界に含まれ、これを'''六欲天'''という。
六欲天を上から記載すると次の通りとなる。
*[[他化自在天]]（たけじざいてん） :欲界の最高位。六欲天の第6天、[[天魔]]波旬の住処。 *[[化楽天]]（けらくてん、楽変化天＝らくへんげてん、とも） :六欲天の第5天。この天に住む者は、自己の対境（五境）を変化して娯楽の境とする。 *[[兜率天]]（とそつてん、覩史多天＝としたてん、とも） :六欲天の第4天。[[須弥山]]の頂上、12[[由旬]]の処にある。 *[[夜摩天]]（やまてん、焔摩天＝えんまてん、とも） :六欲天の第3天。時に随って快楽を受くる世界。 *[[忉利天]]（とうりてん、三十三天＝さんじゅうさんてん、とも） :六欲天の第2天。須弥山の頂上、閻浮提の上、8万由旬の処にある。[[帝釈天]]のいる場所。 *[[四天王|四大王衆天]]（しだいおうしゅてん、[[四天王]]の住む場所） :六欲天の第1天。[[持国天]]・[[増長天]]・[[広目天]]・[[多聞天]]の四天王がいる場所。
== 関連項目 == *[[天魔]]
{{天}} {{buddhism2}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:ろくよくてん}} [[Category:天 (仏教)]] [[Category:名数6|よくてん]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''六欲天'''（ろくよくてん）は、[[天 (仏教)|天上界]]のうち、いまだ欲望に捉われる6つの天界をいう。六天ともいう。またそのうちの最高位・[[他化自在天]]を特に指して言う場合もある{{efn|他化自在天は、[[天魔]]波旬（てんま・はじゅん）の住処であることから、[[織田信長]]は「六欲天の魔王」と自称したといわれる{{要出典|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}。}}。

Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}, and it is said th..."
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}, and it is said that {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|ja|織田信長}} referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{要出典|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Realms of Existence|ja|六道}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界}}, {{ill|Human Realm|ja|人間界}}, and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|en|ja|他化自在天}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|en|ja|化楽天}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|en|ja|兜率天}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|en|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|en|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天}}. * {{ill|Shitenno|en|ja|四大王衆天}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.
== Notes == === Annotations === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist}}
{{天}} {{buddhism2}}
[[Category:Six Heavens of the Desire Realm]] [[Category:Celestial Beings]] [[Category:Three Realms]] [[ja:六欲天]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}, and it is said that {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|ja|織田信長}} referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{要出典|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.

Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界}}.

The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|en|ja|他化自在天}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|en|ja|化楽天}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|en|ja|兜率天}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|en|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|en|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天}}. * {{ill|Shitenno|en|ja|四大王衆天}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.

{{出典の明記|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}, and it is said that {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|ja|織田信長}} referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{要出典|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Realms of Existence|ja|六道}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界}}, {{ill|Human Realm|ja|人間界}}, and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|en|ja|他化自在天}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|en|ja|化楽天}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|en|ja|兜率天}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|en|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|en|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天}}. * {{ill|Shitenno|en|ja|四大王衆天}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.

{{出典の明記|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokutja''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavjas of the [[Heavjaly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavjas. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavjas, the {{ill|Takijizaitja|ja|他化自在天}}{{efn|Takijizaitja is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heavja|ja|天魔波旬}}, and it is said that {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|ja|織田信長}} referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavjas"{{要出典|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Realms of Existjace|ja|六道}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界}}, {{ill|Human Realm|ja|人間界}}, and {{ill|Heavjaly Realms|ja|天上界}}) and the Tja Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界}}. However, the Heavjaly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascjad to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界}}.
Within the Heavjaly Realms, the six lower heavjas nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavjas of Desire''' (Rokuyokutja).
The Six Heavjas of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaitja|ja|ja|他化自在天}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heavja of the Six Heavjas, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heavja|ja|天魔波旬}}. * {{ill|Keratutja|ja|ja|化楽天}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heavja. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of jajoymjat. * {{ill|Tosotsutja|ja|ja|兜率天}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heavja. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamatja|ja|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heavja. A realm where beings experijace pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toritja|ja|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heavja. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天}}. * {{ill|Shitjano|ja|ja|四大王衆天}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heavja. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavjaly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.

{{出典の明記|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}, and it is said that {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{要出典|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Realms of Existence|ja|六道}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界}}, {{ill|Human Realm|ja|人間界}}, and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|ja|兜率天}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天}}. * {{ill|Shitenno|ja|四大王衆天}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.
== Notes == === Annotations === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist}}

Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.

== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Realms of Existence|ja|六道}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界}}, {{ill|Human Realm|ja|人間界}}, and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|ja|兜率天}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天}}. * {{ill|Shitenno|ja|四大王衆天}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.

However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|ja|兜率天}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天}}. * {{ill|Shitenno|ja|四大王衆天}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.

{{出典の明記|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[:en:Devaloka|Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}, and it is said that {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{要出典|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Realms of Existence|ja|六道}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界}}, {{ill|Human Realm|ja|人間界}}, and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|ja|兜率天}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天}}. * {{ill|Shitenno|ja|四大王衆天}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.

== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Realms of Existence|ja|六道}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界}}, {{ill|Human Realm|ja|人間界}}, and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|ja|兜率天}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天}}. * {{ill|Shitenno|ja|四大王衆天}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.

[[Category:Six Heavens of the Desire Realm]] [[Category:Celestial Beings]] [[Category:Three Realms]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Six Desire Heavens-qq]] [[Category:Six Desire Heavens]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Six Desire Heavens-qq]] [[Category:Six Desire Heavens]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Six Desire Heavens]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Six Desire Heavens]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Six Desire Heavens]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Six Desire Heavens]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Six Desire Heavens]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Six Desire Heavens]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Six Desire Heavens]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Six Desire Heavens]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Six Desire Heavens]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:六欲天]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[:en:Devaloka|Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}, and it is said that {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{要出典|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Realms of Existence|ja|六道}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界}}, {{ill|Human Realm|ja|人間界}}, and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|ja|兜率天}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天}}. * {{ill|Shitenno|ja|四大王衆天}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.

Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.

Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|ja|兜率天}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.

{{出典の明記|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[:en:Devaloka|Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}, and it is said that {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{要出典|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Realms of Existence|ja|六道}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界}}, {{ill|Human Realm|ja|人間界}}, and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|ja|兜率天}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天}}. * {{ill|Shitenno|ja|四大王衆天}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.

The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|ja|兜率天}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天}}. * {{ill|Shitenno|ja|四大王衆天}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[:en:Devaloka|Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}, and it is said that {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{要出典|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Realms of Existence|ja|六道}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界}}, {{ill|Human Realm|ja|人間界}}, and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|ja|兜率天}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天}}. * {{ill|Shitenno|ja|四大王衆天}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.
== Notes == === Annotations === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist}}
{{天}} {{buddhism2}}

{{出典の明記|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[:en:Devaloka|Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}, and it is said that {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{要出典|date=2017年10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Realms of Existence|ja|六道}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界}}, {{ill|Human Realm|ja|人間界}}, and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|ja|兜率天}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天}}. * {{ill|Shitenno|ja|四大王衆天}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.
== Notes == === Annotations === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist}}

Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[:en:Devaloka|Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}, and it is said that {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{要出典|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Realms of Existence|ja|六道}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界}}, {{ill|Human Realm|ja|人間界}}, and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|ja|兜率天}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|ja|忉利天}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天}}. * {{ill|Shitenno|ja|四大王衆天}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天}}.
== Notes == === Annotations === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist}}
{{天}} {{buddhism2}}
<!--Six Desire Heavens-->
<!--Six Desire Heavens-->

{{出典の明記|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[:en:Devaloka|Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界|12=draft}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|12=draft}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=draft}}, and it is said that {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft}} referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{要出典|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教|12=draft}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Realms of Existence|ja|六道|12=draft}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|12=draft}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|12=draft}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|12=draft}}, {{ill|Human Realm|ja|人間界|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|12=draft}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|12=draft}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|12=draft}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|12=draft}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界|12=draft}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|12=draft}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|12=draft}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界|12=draft}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|12=draft}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|12=draft}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|ja|兜率天|12=draft}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山|12=draft}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬|12=draft}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天|12=draft}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|ja|忉利天|12=draft}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shitenno|ja|四大王衆天|12=draft}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王|12=draft}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天|12=draft}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天|12=draft}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|12=draft}}.

Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Desire Realm|qq=}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|qq=}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|qq=}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Realms|qq=}}.

The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tosotsuten|ja|兜率天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tosotsuten|qq=}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Sumeru|qq=}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yojana|qq=}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaten|qq=}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|ja|忉利天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriten|qq=}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra|qq=}}. * {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|lt=Four Heavenly Kings|lt=Shitenno|ja|四大王衆天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitenno|qq=}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Heavenly Kings|qq=}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dhṛtarāṣṭra|qq=}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Virūḍhaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Virūpākṣa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|qq=}}.

{{出典の明記|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[:en:Devaloka|Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/天上界|qq=}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=}}, and it is said that {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}} referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|ja|仏教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhism|qq=}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Realms of Existence|ja|六道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Realms of Existence|qq=}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|qq=}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|qq=}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|qq=}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|qq=}}, {{ill|Human Realm|ja|人間界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Human Realm|qq=}}, and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|qq=}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|qq=}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|qq=}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|qq=}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|qq=}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire Realm|ja|欲界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Desire Realm|qq=}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|qq=}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|qq=}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Three Realms|ja|三界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Realms|qq=}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tushita|lt=Tushita|lt=Tosotsuten|ja|兜率天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tosotsuten|qq=}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Sumeru|ja|須弥山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Sumeru|qq=}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|ja|由旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yojana|qq=}} high. * {{ill|Yamaten|ja|夜摩天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaten|qq=}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Toriten|ja|忉利天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriten|qq=}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Indra|ja|帝釈天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra|qq=}}. * {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|lt=Four Heavenly Kings|lt=Shitenno|ja|四大王衆天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitenno|qq=}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|ja|四天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Heavenly Kings|qq=}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|ja|持国天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dhṛtarāṣṭra|qq=}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka|ja|増長天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Virūḍhaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|ja|広目天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Virūpākṣa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|qq=}}.

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[:en:Devaloka|Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/天上界|qq=}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=}}, and it is said that [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the worldview includes the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]] ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|qq=}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|qq=}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|qq=}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|qq=}}, [[:en:Human beings in Buddhism|Human Realm]], and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|qq=}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|qq=}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|qq=}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|qq=}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|qq=}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|qq=}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|qq=}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|qq=}}.

It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=}}, and it is said that [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the worldview includes the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]] ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|qq=}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|qq=}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|qq=}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|qq=}}, [[:en:Human beings in Buddhism|Human Realm]], and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|qq=}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|qq=}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|qq=}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|qq=}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|qq=}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|qq=}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|qq=}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|qq=}}.
== Notes == === Annotations === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist}}

The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天}}.

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Six Heavens of the Desire Realm
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[:en:Devaloka|Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/天上界|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and it is said that [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the worldview includes the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]] ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Human beings in Buddhism|Human Realm]], and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|qq=}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天}}.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Six Desire Heavens" to "six heavens of the desire realm"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11392421]] Wikidata name: six heavens of the desire realm [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{出典の明記|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[:en:Devaloka|Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/天上界|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and it is said that [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the worldview includes the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]] ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Human beings in Buddhism|Human Realm]], and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|qq=}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天}}.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11392421]] Wikidata name: six heavens of the desire realm [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{出典の明記|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[:en:Devaloka|Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/天上界|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)}}) that remain bound by desires. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and it is said that [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.

Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|qq=}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].

The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven.
