It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and it is said that [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the worldview includes the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]] ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Human beings in Buddhism|Human Realm]], and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|qq=|1=Rūpadhātu|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Formless Realm}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata}}.

It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and it is said that [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the worldview includes the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]] ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Human beings in Buddhism|Human Realm]], and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|qq=|1=Rūpadhātu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843720}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Formless Realm|WD=Q837896}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.

It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and it is said that [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the worldview includes the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]] ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Human beings in Buddhism|Human Realm]], and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|qq=|1=Rūpadhātu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843720}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Formless Realm|WD=Q837896}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.

It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and it is said that [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the worldview includes the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]] ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Human beings in Buddhism|Human Realm]], and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|1=Rūpadhātu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843720}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Formless Realm|WD=Q837896}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.

It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and it is said that [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the worldview includes the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]] ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Human beings in Buddhism|Human Realm]], and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|1=Rūpadhātu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843720}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Formless Realm|WD=Q837896}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.

== Overview == In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the worldview includes the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]] ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Human beings in Buddhism|Human Realm]], and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|1=Rūpadhātu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843720}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Formless Realm|WD=Q837896}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六欲天]][[ko:육욕천]][[my:နတ်ပြည် ၆ ထပ်]][[th:ฉกามาพจร]][[zh:欲界天]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392421]] Wikidata name: six heavens of the desire realm [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[:en:Devaloka|Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/天上界|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and it is said that [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the worldview includes the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]] ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Human beings in Buddhism|Human Realm]], and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|1=Rūpadhātu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843720}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Formless Realm|WD=Q837896}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:

It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.

It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六欲天]][[ko:육욕천]][[my:နတ်ပြည် ၆ ထပ်]][[th:ฉกามาพจร]][[zh:欲界天]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392421]] Wikidata name: six heavens of the desire realm [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[:en:Devaloka|Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/天上界|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and it is said that [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the worldview includes the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]] ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Human beings in Buddhism|Human Realm]], and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|1=Rūpadhātu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843720}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Formless Realm|WD=Q837896}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六欲天]][[ko:육욕천]][[my:နတ်ပြည် ၆ ထပ်]][[th:ฉกามาพจร]][[zh:欲界天]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392421]] Wikidata name: six heavens of the desire realm [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the [[:en:Devaloka|Heavenly Realms]] ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/天上界|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and it is said that [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the worldview includes the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]] ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Human beings in Buddhism|Human Realm]], and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|1=Rūpadhātu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843720}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Formless Realm|WD=Q837896}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.

It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and it is said that [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the worldview includes the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]] ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Human beings in Buddhism|Human Realm]], and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|1=Rūpadhātu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843720}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Formless Realm|WD=Q837896}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.

== Overview == In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the worldview includes the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]] ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Human beings in Buddhism|Human Realm]], and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|1=Rūpadhātu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843720}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Formless Realm|WD=Q837896}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.

It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and it is said that [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], the worldview includes the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]] ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Human beings in Buddhism|Human Realm]], and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]]. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|1=Rūpadhātu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843720}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Formless Realm|WD=Q837896}}, collectively referred to as the [[:en:Trailokya|Three Realms]].
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * [[:en:Tushita|Tosotsuten]] (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop [[:en:Mount Meru|Mount Sumeru]], 12 [[:en:Yojana|Yojana]] high. * [[:en:Yāma|Yamaten]] (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Toriten]] (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of [[:en:Śakra (Buddhism)|Indra]]. * [[:en:Cāturmahārājakāyika|Shitenno]] (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the [[:en:Four Heavenly Kings|Four Heavenly Kings]]: [[:en:Dhṛtarāṣṭra|Dhṛtarāṣṭra]], [[:en:Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|Virūḍhaka]], [[:en:Virūpākṣa|Virūpākṣa]], and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.

It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and it is said that {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Paths|en|Six Paths|WD=Q1192450|lt=Six Realms of Existence}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Human beings in Buddhism|en|Human beings in Buddhism|WD=Q5937705|lt=Human Realm}}, and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire realm|en|Desire realm|WD=Q4447630|lt=Desire Realm}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|1=Rūpadhātu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843720}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Formless Realm|WD=Q837896}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Trailokya|en|Trailokya|WD=Q1156452|lt=Three Realms}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tushita|en|Tushita|WD=Q660582|lt=Tosotsuten}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Meru|en|Mount Meru|WD=Q652550|lt=Mount Sumeru}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|en|Yojana|WD=Q2416457|lt=Yojana}} high. * {{ill|Yāma|en|Yāma|WD=Q11746495|lt=Yamaten}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Trāyastriṃśa|en|Trāyastriṃśa|WD=Q1360289|lt=Toriten}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Śakra (Buddhism)|en|Śakra (Buddhism)|WD=Q1444745|lt=Indra}}. * {{ill|Cāturmahārājakāyika|en|Cāturmahārājakāyika|WD=Q65085497|lt=Shitenno}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|en|Four Heavenly Kings|WD=Q1068367|lt=Four Heavenly Kings}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|en|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|WD=Q1188767|lt=Dhṛtarāṣṭra}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|en|Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|WD=Q1188515|lt=Virūḍhaka}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|en|Virūpākṣa|WD=Q1188714|lt=Virūpākṣa}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.

{{Referencesの明記|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the {{ill|Devaloka|en|Devaloka|WD=Q2365892|lt=Heavenly Realms}} ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/天上界|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) that remain bound by desires. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Śakra (Buddhism)|en|Śakra (Buddhism)|WD=Q1444745|lt=Indra}}. * {{ill|Cāturmahārājakāyika|en|Cāturmahārājakāyika|WD=Q65085497|lt=Shitenno}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|en|Four Heavenly Kings|WD=Q1068367|lt=Four Heavenly Kings}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|en|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|WD=Q1188767|lt=Dhṛtarāṣṭra}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|en|Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|WD=Q1188515|lt=Virūḍhaka}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|en|Virūpākṣa|WD=Q1188714|lt=Virūpākṣa}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六欲天]][[ko:육욕천]][[my:နတ်ပြည် ၆ ထပ်]][[th:ฉกามาพจร]][[zh:欲界天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六欲天]][[ko:육욕천]][[my:နတ်ပြည် ၆ ထပ်]][[th:ฉกามาพจร]][[zh:欲界天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六欲天]][[ko:육욕천]][[my:နတ်ပြည် ၆ ထပ်]][[th:ฉกามาพจร]][[zh:欲界天]]
{{Referencesの明記|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the {{ill|Devaloka|en|Devaloka|WD=Q2365892|lt=Heavenly Realms}} ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/天上界|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and it is said that {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Paths|en|Six Paths|WD=Q1192450|lt=Six Realms of Existence}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Human beings in Buddhism|en|Human beings in Buddhism|WD=Q5937705|lt=Human Realm}}, and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire realm|en|Desire realm|WD=Q4447630|lt=Desire Realm}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|1=Rūpadhātu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843720}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Formless Realm|WD=Q837896}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Trailokya|en|Trailokya|WD=Q1156452|lt=Three Realms}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tushita|en|Tushita|WD=Q660582|lt=Tosotsuten}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Meru|en|Mount Meru|WD=Q652550|lt=Mount Sumeru}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|en|Yojana|WD=Q2416457|lt=Yojana}} high. * {{ill|Yāma|en|Yāma|WD=Q11746495|lt=Yamaten}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Trāyastriṃśa|en|Trāyastriṃśa|WD=Q1360289|lt=Toriten}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Śakra (Buddhism)|en|Śakra (Buddhism)|WD=Q1444745|lt=Indra}}. * {{ill|Cāturmahārājakāyika|en|Cāturmahārājakāyika|WD=Q65085497|lt=Shitenno}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|en|Four Heavenly Kings|WD=Q1068367|lt=Four Heavenly Kings}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|en|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|WD=Q1188767|lt=Dhṛtarāṣṭra}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|en|Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|WD=Q1188515|lt=Virūḍhaka}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|en|Virūpākṣa|WD=Q1188714|lt=Virūpākṣa}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.

* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tushita|en|Tushita|WD=Q660582|lt=Tosotsuten|1=Tushita}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Meru|en|Mount Meru|WD=Q652550|lt=Mount Sumeru|1=Mount Meru}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|en|Yojana|WD=Q2416457|lt=Yojana|1=Yojana}} high. * {{ill|Yāma|en|Yāma|WD=Q11746495|lt=Yamaten|1=Yāma}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Trāyastriṃśa|en|Trāyastriṃśa|WD=Q1360289|lt=Toriten|1=Trāyastriṃśa}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Śakra (Buddhism)|en|Śakra (Buddhism)|WD=Q1444745|lt=Indra|1=Śakra (Buddhism)}}. * {{ill|Cāturmahārājakāyika|en|Cāturmahārājakāyika|WD=Q65085497|lt=Shitenno|1=Cāturmahārājakāyika}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|en|Four Heavenly Kings|WD=Q1068367|lt=Four Heavenly Kings|1=Four Heavenly Kings}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|en|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|WD=Q1188767|lt=Dhṛtarāṣṭra|1=Dhṛtarāṣṭra}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|en|Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|WD=Q1188515|lt=Virūḍhaka|1=Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|en|Virūpākṣa|WD=Q1188714|lt=Virūpākṣa|1=Virūpākṣa}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六欲天]][[ko:육욕천]][[my:နတ်ပြည် ၆ ထပ်]][[th:ฉกามาพจร]][[zh:欲界天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六欲天]][[ko:육욕천]][[my:နတ်ပြည် ၆ ထပ်]][[th:ฉกามาพจร]][[zh:欲界天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六欲天]][[ko:육욕천]][[my:နတ်ပြည် ၆ ထပ်]][[th:ฉกามาพจร]][[zh:欲界天]]
{{Referencesの明記|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)}} '''Rokuyokuten''' (六欲天) refers to the six heavens of the {{ill|Devaloka|en|Devaloka|WD=Q2365892|lt=Heavenly Realms|1=Devaloka}} ({{ill|天上界|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/天上界|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) that remain bound by desires. It is also known as the Six Heavens. Sometimes, it specifically refers to the highest of these heavens, the {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|Takijizaiten is the dwelling place of the {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and it is said that {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}} referred to himself as the "Demon King of the Six Heavens"{{Citation needed|date=2017th year 10月14日 (土) 10:04 (UTC)|title=}}.}}.
== Overview == In {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}}, the worldview includes the {{ill|Six Paths|en|Six Paths|WD=Q1192450|lt=Six Realms of Existence|1=Six Paths}} ({{ill|Hell|ja|地獄界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hell|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hungry Ghosts|ja|餓鬼界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hungry Ghosts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Animals|ja|畜生界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Animals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Asuras|ja|修羅界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuras|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Human beings in Buddhism|en|Human beings in Buddhism|WD=Q5937705|lt=Human Realm|1=Human beings in Buddhism}}, and {{ill|Heavenly Realms|ja|天上界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天 (仏教)|1=Devaloka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2365892}}) and the Ten Realms (adding the {{ill|Realm of Śrāvakas|ja|声聞界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Śrāvakas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja|縁覚界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Pratyekabuddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja|菩薩界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Bodhisattvas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Realm of Buddhas|ja|仏界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Realm of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Six Realms).
Of these, the realms from Hell to the Human Realm are worlds bound by desires, collectively called the {{ill|Desire realm|en|Desire realm|WD=Q4447630|lt=Desire Realm|1=Desire realm}}. However, the Heavenly Realms are divided into finer gradations, where the higher levels gradually detach from desires and ascend to the material {{ill|Form Realm|ja|色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Form Realm|1=Rūpadhātu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843720}} and the spiritual {{ill|Formless Realm|ja|無色界|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formless Realm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Formless Realm|WD=Q837896}}, collectively referred to as the {{ill|Trailokya|en|Trailokya|WD=Q1156452|lt=Three Realms|1=Trailokya}}.
Within the Heavenly Realms, the six lower heavens nearest to the Human Realm are still bound by desires and are thus included in the Desire Realm, collectively known as the '''Six Heavens of Desire''' (Rokuyokuten).
The Six Heavens of Desire, listed from highest to lowest, are as follows:
* {{ill|Takijizaiten|ja|他化自在天|1=Takijizaiten|2=ja|3=Takijizaiten|4=zh|5=他化自在天|6=th|7=ปรนิมมิตวสวัตดี|8=ru|9=Паринимитра-вашавартин|10=ပရနိမ္မိတဝသဝတ္တီဘုံ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijizaiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (たけじざいてん) : The highest of the Desire Realm. It is the sixth heaven of the Six Heavens, the dwelling place of {{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Keratuten|ja|化楽天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keratuten|qq=}} (けらくてん, also known as 楽変化天) : The fifth heaven. The beings of this realm transform their surroundings into sources of enjoyment. * {{ill|Tushita|en|Tushita|WD=Q660582|lt=Tosotsuten|1=Tushita}} (とそつてん, also known as 覩史多天) : The fourth heaven. Located atop {{ill|Mount Meru|en|Mount Meru|WD=Q652550|lt=Mount Sumeru|1=Mount Meru}}, 12 {{ill|Yojana|en|Yojana|WD=Q2416457|lt=Yojana|1=Yojana}} high. * {{ill|Yāma|en|Yāma|WD=Q11746495|lt=Yamaten|1=Yāma}} (やまてん, also known as 焔摩天) : The third heaven. A realm where beings experience pleasure according to time. * {{ill|Trāyastriṃśa|en|Trāyastriṃśa|WD=Q1360289|lt=Toriten|1=Trāyastriṃśa}} (とうりてん, also known as 三十三天) : The second heaven. Located at the summit of Mount Sumeru, above Jambudvīpa, 80,000 yojanas high, and the dwelling place of {{ill|Śakra (Buddhism)|en|Śakra (Buddhism)|WD=Q1444745|lt=Indra|1=Śakra (Buddhism)}}. * {{ill|Cāturmahārājakāyika|en|Cāturmahārājakāyika|WD=Q65085497|lt=Shitenno|1=Cāturmahārājakāyika}} (しだいおうしゅてん, also known as 四天王衆天) : The first heaven. Home to the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|en|Four Heavenly Kings|WD=Q1068367|lt=Four Heavenly Kings|1=Four Heavenly Kings}}: {{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|en|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|WD=Q1188767|lt=Dhṛtarāṣṭra|1=Dhṛtarāṣṭra}}, {{ill|Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|en|Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|WD=Q1188515|lt=Virūḍhaka|1=Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)}}, {{ill|Virūpākṣa|en|Virūpākṣa|WD=Q1188714|lt=Virūpākṣa|1=Virūpākṣa}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|ja|多聞天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vaiśravaṇa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毘沙門天|1=Vaiśravaṇa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866315}}.
